novel_features.name: Powder Day Scorer
novel_features.description: Rate upcoming days 1-10 for powder quality by combining forecast snow totals, wind, temperature, and historical averages
novel_features.rationale: Requires correlating forecast data with cached historical averages that only exist in the local SQLite store
novel_features.why_it_matters: When an agent needs to recommend the single best day to ski this week, this command gives a scored ranking instead of raw forecast numbers

novel_features.name: Multi-Resort Powder Ranker
novel_features.description: Rank all synced resorts by powder potential, combining expected snowfall, base depth, and terrain openness
novel_features.rationale: Requires cross-resort aggregation of forecast and report data that no single API call provides
novel_features.why_it_matters: When an agent needs to answer 'where should I ski this weekend in Colorado', this gives a ranked list with scores

novel_features.name: Storm Tracker
novel_features.description: Show storm progression — when snow starts, peaks, and ends — by correlating rolling hourly forecasts over time
novel_features.rationale: Requires time-series analysis of cached hourly forecast snapshots that only exist locally
novel_features.why_it_matters: When planning travel around a storm, this shows exactly when to arrive and when the storm window closes

novel_features.name: Favorites Dashboard
novel_features.description: One-command view of all favorited locations with current temp, 24h snow, 5-day total, and operating status
novel_features.rationale: Requires local favorites list and parallel API calls across multiple locations joined in one view
novel_features.why_it_matters: Replaces opening the OpenSnow app — one command shows everything an agent needs about the user's preferred mountains

novel_features.name: Overnight Snowfall Check
novel_features.description: Check the semi-daily forecast for the overnight period at favorited resorts — the powder hunter's morning ritual
novel_features.rationale: Extracts the overnight (6pm-6am) segment from semi-daily forecasts and presents it as a simple answer
novel_features.why_it_matters: The first question every skier asks: 'How much snow fell overnight?' This answers it for all favorites in one call

novel_features.name: Conditions Diff
novel_features.description: Compare current snow report against the last-synced version to see what changed: new snow, lifts opened or closed, status changes
novel_features.rationale: Requires comparing current API data against previously cached snapshots in SQLite
novel_features.why_it_matters: Agents monitoring resort conditions can detect meaningful changes without parsing raw reports

novel_features.name: Daily Snow Digest
novel_features.description: Pull all Daily Snow posts for favorited regions, strip HTML to clean text, and show a summary digest
novel_features.rationale: Aggregates multiple Daily Snow posts across regions and renders HTML content as readable terminal text
novel_features.why_it_matters: Expert forecasts from OpenSnow meteorologists, rendered for agents and terminals instead of requiring a browser

novel_features.name: Historical Snowfall Trends
novel_features.description: Show snowfall trends, season totals vs averages, and base depth progression from cached report snapshots
novel_features.rationale: Requires accumulated historical snapshots in SQLite — the API only returns current data
novel_features.why_it_matters: When planning a trip weeks out, historical context shows whether the mountain is trending up or down

narrative.headline: Every OpenSnow forecast, snow report, and Daily Snow post — plus powder scoring, storm tracking, and historical trends no other tool has.
narrative.value_prop: The OpenSnow CLI puts the same hyper-local mountain forecasts trusted by millions of skiers directly into your terminal. Sync resort data to a local SQLite store, then score powder days, rank resorts, and track storms offline. The Daily Snow digest brings expert meteorologist forecasts to your terminal without opening a browser.
narrative.auth_narrative: OpenSnow API access is partnership-only. Set your API key with `opensnow-pp-cli config set-token <key>`. The key is stored locally and passed as a query parameter on every request. Access level 5 covers point-based forecasts; level 10 unlocks named locations, snow reports, and Daily Snow.

quickstart.comment: Set your OpenSnow API key (partnership access required)
Verify API key is valid and API is reachable
Read the Colorado Daily Snow forecast

quickstart.command: opensnow-pp-cli favorites add alta steamboat telluride

troubleshoots.fix: Run `opensnow-pp-cli config set-token <key>` — the API key must be set before any request
Your API key has access level 5 — named-location endpoints require level 10. Contact hello@opensnow.com to upgrade.
Resort may be closed for the season. Check status_display field — 'Closed' or 'Summer Operations' means no active snow data.

troubleshoots.symptom: No results for a location slug
troubleshoots.fix: Slugs are case-sensitive and specific. Try common names: alta, steamboat, aspenhighlands. Email hello@opensnow.com for the full slug list.

narrative.when_to_use: Use the OpenSnow CLI when you need mountain weather forecasts with snow-specific detail — day/night snowfall splits, powder quality, and resort operating status. Ideal for trip planning agents that need to compare resorts, track storms, or answer 'where should I ski this weekend?' The Daily Snow digest provides expert meteorologist analysis not available from generic weather APIs.

recipes.title: Best powder day this week
recipes.explanation: Ranks all synced Colorado resorts by powder potential for the next 5 days, returning only key fields for agent consumption

recipes.title: Morning snow check
recipes.explanation: Quick check of overnight snowfall at all favorited resorts — the powder hunter's morning ritual

recipes.title: Compare two resorts
recipes.explanation: Side-by-side comparison of forecasts and conditions for trip planning

recipes.title: Expert forecast digest
recipes.explanation: Read today's Daily Snow from OpenSnow's meteorologists in terminal-friendly format

recipes.title: Storm watch
recipes.explanation: Track when the next storm starts, peaks, and ends at Telluride with narrowed fields for agent context
